Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> It may help, but I cannot guarantee that others lproc will NOT export un-initizliaed things too early. Changing the initialization orders may cause potential trouble. That is why I did not do that.
sounds like you're feeling lazy to check the code ;)
btw, during unlink we call osd_write() at least 4 times. and we want to run something like 100K+ unlinks/sec

Patch Set 1:

I did checked, otherwise, I will not say that such way will export non-initialized things too earlier. But because they are lproc interfaces, anyone can see that after exported. I cannot say that nobody will access related lproc during the mount process. Further more, if someone add new OSD lproc interface in the future, he/she may assume that osd-ldislfs and osd-zfs use the same initialization order, then not realize that the system is under non-initialized status.

> btw, during unlink we call osd_write() at least 4 times. and we want to run something like 100K+ unlinks/sec

I am thinking whether we can register some simplified (without IO statistics) osd_body_ops for oi_scrub object?

parents.subject: LU-5991 obd: fix mount error handing

commit.subject: LU-10607 osd-zfs: skip io stat for OI scrub
commit.message:
LU-10607 osd-zfs: skip io stat for OI scrub

It is unnecessary to stat io for OI scrub triggered request.
On the other hand, the OI setup logic may read/write the OI
scrub file. At that time, related lproc (including io stat)
for such OSD is not initialized yet. So this patch skips io
stat for OI scrub.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I9498c1351c1875ac9aa46eed5189cb61a6d102ac
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/31180
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
